name: DJ Set Clipper
description: Extract short clips from DJ sets and music events in Google Drive. Auto-detects peak moments via audio analysis. Posts go to TikTok drafts (SELF_ONLY) so you can swap music before publishing.
requiredTools: gws CLI (Google Workspace — for Drive access)

crons.name: schedule-day
crons.description: Schedule queued clips for today via Dropspace (after you've added clips to the queue)

setupSteps: Get a Dropspace API key at https://dropspace.dev → Settings → API Keys
Set up Google Workspace credentials for Drive access (see SETUP.md in this template)
Install ffmpeg: brew install ffmpeg (macOS) or apt install ffmpeg (Linux)
Edit app.json — replace YOUR_GOOGLE_DRIVE_FOLDER_ID with your actual Drive folder IDs
Edit CONTEXT.md — fill in your event name, venue, and artist handles
Review the generated clips, then queue them manually or via the clipper script
Set up schedule-day cron to auto-schedule queued clips each morning
notes: TikTok posts are SELF_ONLY by default — they go to your drafts so you can add music before publishing
Peak detection uses audio amplitude analysis to find the most energetic moments
Clips default to 60 seconds — tweak clipDurationSec in app.json
See ~/markus/clipper/ for the full clipper documentation
